name: Chocolate Nut Slices
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F (180°C).
2. In a large bowl, combine the Golden Crisco shortening, granulated sugar, brown sugar, milk, and vanilla extract. Mix at medium speed of an electric mixer until well blended. Add the egg.
3. In another bowl, mix together flour, cocoa powder, baking soda, and salt. Gradually add this mixture to the creamed mixture. Beat until just combined. Stir in pecans and chocolate chips.
4. Divide the dough into 4 equal portions. Shape each portion into a roll about 1 x 8 inches (2.5 x 20 cm) long on waxed paper. Place the rolls 3 inches apart on an ungreased baking sheet.
5. Bake for 10 minutes. Allow to cool on the baking sheet.
6. For drizzle, melt white coating chocolate and 1/2 teaspoon Golden Crisco shortening over low heat or at 50% power in a microwave. Stir until smooth. Drizzle over cooled cookies, back and forth from spoon. Sprinkle with chopped pecans before chocolate hardens, if desired.
7. Cut the rolls diagonally into 1-inch (2.5 cm) slices. Makes: 40 slices. Note: To speed up chocolate setting, place cookies in refrigerator for a few minutes. TIP: For even baking results, use shiny, heavy-gauge aluminum baking sheets and keep all cookies on the same sheet approximately the same size.
